ddls:
CREATE TABLE "characters" (
  "character_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each character */,
  "character_name" TEXT /* Name of the character */,
  "character_role" TEXT /* Role or class of the character (e.g., Knight, Mage) */,
  "personality_traits" TEXT /* Personality characteristics of the character (e.g., brave, sarcastic) */,
  "emotional_context" TEXT /* Default emotional context of the character (e.g., friendly, aggressive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("character_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "character_progress" (
  "progress_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each progress entry */,
  "character_id" INTEGER /* ID of the character associated with the progress */,
  "player_id" INTEGER /* ID of the player influencing the character progress */,
  "progress_date" TEXT /* Date of the progress update */,
  "progress_level" INTEGER /* Current progress level of the character */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("progress_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_character_progress_player_id FOREIGN KEY ("player_id") REFERENCES players ("player_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_character_progress_character_id FOREIGN KEY ("character_id") REFERENCES characters ("character_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "dialogue_responses" (
  "response_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each response */,
  "character_id" INTEGER /* Identifier referencing the character associated with the response */,
  "response_type" TEXT /* Type of response (e.g., Praise, Thank You, Apologize, Farewell) */,
  "response_text" TEXT /* Text of the response */,
  "response_category" TEXT /* Category of the response (e.g., positive, negative, neutral) */,
  "response_emotional_context" TEXT /* Emotional context of the response (e.g., happy, sad, angry) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("response_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_dialogue_responses_character_id FOREIGN KEY ("character_id") REFERENCES characters ("character_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "response_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each response type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the response type (e.g., Praise, Thank You) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the response type */,
  "response_category" TEXT /* Category of the response type (e.g., positive, negative, neutral) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "response_analytics" (
  "analytics_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analytics entry */,
  "response_id" INTEGER /* ID of the response associated with the analytics */,
  "response_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the response has been used */,
  "player_engagement_score" REAL /* Player engagement score for the response (e.g., 1.0 - 5.0) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analytics_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_response_analytics_response_id FOREIGN KEY ("response_id") REFERENCES dialogue_responses ("response_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "player_interactions" (
  "interaction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each interaction */,
  "character_id" INTEGER /* ID of the character involved in the interaction */,
  "response_id" INTEGER /* ID of the response given by the character */,
  "player_id" INTEGER /* ID of the player initiating the interaction */,
  "interaction_date" TEXT /* Date of the interaction */,
  "player_emotional_context" TEXT /* Emotional context of the player during the interaction (e.g., happy, sad, angry) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("interaction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_player_interactions_player_id FOREIGN KEY ("player_id") REFERENCES players ("player_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_player_interactions_response_id FOREIGN KEY ("response_id") REFERENCES dialogue_responses ("response_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_player_interactions_character_id FOREIGN KEY ("character_id") REFERENCES characters ("character_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "players" (
  "player_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each player */,
  "player_name" TEXT /* Name of the player */,
  "player_level" INTEGER /* Current level of the player */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("player_id")
)
